title: Lack of Observability
summary: The system was not designed with sufficient logging, metrics, tracing, and diagnosability.
inspection_goal: Identify architectural areas where diagnosing behavior or failure would be difficult due to missing visibility points.
why_it_matters: Makes system behavior harder to understand
primary_signals: Critical paths with no explicit telemetry or metric nodes
Complex dependency chains with few diagnosable boundaries
Failure-prone regions with weak instrumentation hints
Operationally central components lacking visibility edges
inspection_questions: Which critical paths are hardest to observe?
Where would root-cause analysis likely stall?
Are central dependencies backed by metrics, logs, or traceable boundaries?
Do failure hotspots align with poor diagnosability?
evidence_to_collect: Presence or absence of observability-related nodes or integrations
Incident adjacency to weakly instrumented regions
Context from code patterns around logging, tracing, or metrics
recommendation_patterns: Add telemetry around critical paths and chokepoints
Instrument cross-boundary calls and data flows
Improve trace continuity across major dependencies
Make operationally central components diagnosable by default

severity_guidance.high: Critical architectural paths are hard to diagnose and lack visibility anchors.
severity_guidance.medium: Observability gaps exist around important but not system-wide paths.
severity_guidance.low: Most important regions appear diagnosable with only minor blind spots.
